title: Annual Report on Tax Exemptions for Medicinal Cannabis
summary: Chapter 920 of 2024 (AB 2555, Quirk-Silva) renewed an existing tax exemption for certain donations of medical cannabis products. The law directs our office to submit an annual report containing data on three outcomes related to the exemptions: the number of medicinal cannabis patients served, the amount of medicinal cannabis products donated, and the amount of tax revenue lost. This report fulfills that statutory requirement for 2025.

title: #GerofitExercise: Total Body Routine
summary:
This #GerofitExercise session is a 23-minute total body routine designed to build a strong base for health and mobility. Led by Recreational Therapists Bobby Mclerran and Aaron Degrisellas, this routine focuses on essential movements of strength and flexibility that encompass the entire body. These are suitable for individuals of all abilities and can be performed with little to no equipmentDeveloping a foundational fitness habit is vital for your physical, mental, and emotional health, especially as you age. Join our Gerofit team for a great workout today! If you find this video helpful, please share it with family and friends, and be sure to check out our other #Gerofitexercise videos.

Video Chapters:
00:00 Veteran Testimonial
00:29 Disclaimer
01:04 Introduction
02:24 Warm Up
06:39 Strength First Round
15:34 Strength Second Round
26:32 Cardio Section
33:18 Balance/3rd Round
38:24 Cool Down

title: Bringing VA Care Into the Home
summary:
VA Remote Patient Monitoring – Home Telehealth brings care directly into Veterans’ homes. With VA-provided equipment, active monitoring, and dedicated care coordination, Veterans can track their health, stay connected to their care teams, and receive timely support. 

Pamela South, RPM-HT Lead, shares how Remote Patient Monitoring – Home Telehealth helps Veterans manage chronic conditions, mental health needs, and other health concerns from home while staying connected to VA care teams.For more information, visit: https://www.va.gov/palo-alto-health-care/programs/remote-patient-monitoring-home-telehealth-rpm-ht/

title: Game changer: California Energy Commissioner Nancy Skinner
summary:
CAPITOL WEEKLY PODCAST: If you have noticed that the state now has less "rolling" blackouts, former state senator Nancy Skinner's AB 2514 is a big part of the reason. Skinner is serving her first term on the California Energy Commission after serving as a state senator and member of the Assembly. Her legislation created requirements for electrical energy storage systems - she spoke with us about AB 2514, about the Trump Administration's opposition to California's climate goals, the challenges (and opportunities) presented by Data Centers, and her landmark Name Image and Likeness legislation, SB 206, which changed college sports across the country. And, with the Primary behind us, we have a long list of candidates for Who Had the Worst Week in California Politics.

Continue reading the full article: Capitol Weekly

title: VMA-223 celebrates sundown as Marine Corps’ final Harrier squadron

The ceremony marked an important moment in time for VMA-223 and also signaled the end of an era for Marine Corps aviation as the service continues its transition to an all-5th generation tactical aircraft fleet.

title: 24th Marine Expeditionary Unit Assumes the Watch as Littoral Combat Force-24, Advancing Operation Southern Spear in USSOUTHCOM AOR
summary: The 24th Marine Expeditionary Unit has officially assumed the mission as the premier tactical force-in-readiness within the U.S. Southern Command Area of Responsibility. Operating under the designation of Littoral Combat Force-24, the command offers tactical options via an agile Marine Air-Ground Task Force of over 1,300 Marines and Sailors to the theater. Commanded by U.S. Marine Corps Col. Ryan Lynch, LCF-24 is directly nested under Joint Force Maritime Component Command and Joint Task Force 84-2 to support Operation Southern Spear.

title: PPIC Statewide Survey: Californians and Their Government
summary: Key findings of the survey include: Democrat Xavier Becerra and Republican Steve Hilton lead in the top-two gubernatorial primary. A majority of likely voters support a one-time tax on billionaires, while they are split on setting new voter ID requirements. Majorities say that higher gas prices have affected their financial situation and that military actions in Iran will hurt their finances.

summary: PPIC’s latest survey tracks voter preferences in the gubernatorial primary and the congressional election. Associate survey director Dean Bonner will outline key findings and answer audience questions.

summary:

We’ve Always Done It This Way! on The Auditor.

source: Publication Archive - Public Policy Institute of California
title: Examining Trends in California’s Birth Rates
summary: As in other states, California’s birth rates have fallen to their lowest levels on record, largely driven by fewer births among younger women. The implications of this change are far-reaching: a smaller population may hasten K–12 enrollment declines and strain the state’s safety net while easing pressure on housing and infrastructure demands.

source: Publication Archive - Public Policy Institute of California
title: Policy Brief: Examining Trends in California’s Birth Rates
summary: Birth rates in California have shifted—dropping notably among younger women and rising slightly among older women. California’s decline is slightly larger than the national average, and the change carries positive and negative implications for the state. Thoughtful policy will be needed to plan for a future with a smaller population.

title: CIA Insider: Why the Iran Ceasefire is Extremely Dangerous
summary:
Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis breaks down the fragile new ceasefire with Iran and why the negotiated terms should worry every American.

In this hard-hitting episode, Sam reveals:
• Why the current Iran ceasefire is far more dangerous than it looks
• Iran’s true intentions driven by radical religious beliefs
• The hidden nuclear weapons strategy that changes everything
• How civilian intelligence and military operators actually work together
• The incredible story of American airmen recovered from a hostile environment

From fragile diplomatic deals to high-stakes nuclear brinkmanship and real-world rescue operations — this is unfiltered insight from a career CIA veteran who has seen it all.

Timestamps:
00:00 Ceasefire with Iran
11:00 Nuclear Weapons and Strategy
17:29 Recovery of Airmen

Guest: Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis
